title: Ultrafast Sliding Ferroelectric Switching in Bilayer Hexagonal Boron Nitride Revealed by Deep Learning Molecular Dynamics
authors: Yinan Wang, Poyen Chen, Teruyasu Mizoguchi
abstract: Sliding ferroelectricity in bilayer hexagonal boron nitride (h-BN) offers compelling prospects for next-generation non-volatile memory, yet the atomistic dynamics of electric-field-driven polarization switching remain poorly understood. Here, we present a fully data-driven, coupled atomistic framework that integrates a fine-tuned MACE machine learning potential (MLP) with an equivariant graph convolutional neural network (EGCNN) for real-time Born effective charge (BEC) prediction, enabling large-scale non-equilibrium molecular dynamics simulations of AB-stacked bilayer h-BN under applied electric fields. By implementing a rigorous real-space path-integral polarization formalism combined with a state-constrained Gaussian convolution background extraction procedure, we successfully isolate the intrinsic spontaneous polarization from the dominant dielectric background. Our simulations reveal that coherent single-domain rigid sliding, completing within 5 ps, constitutes a physically viable ultrafast switching mechanism, and reproduces clean ferroelectric hysteresis loops whose shape is qualitatively consistent with experimental observations.
summary: This paper uses machine learning-enhanced molecular dynamics to simulate the switching of ferroelectricity in bilayer h-BN. It demonstrates that polarization switching occurs via an extremely fast, 5-picosecond rigid sliding process. This work provides a computational bridge between atomistic dynamics and experimental observations of ferroelectric hysteresis.
main_problem: Understanding the atomistic dynamics of electric-field-driven polarization switching in sliding ferroelectric bilayer h-BN.
main_result: The study reveals an ultrafast switching mechanism consisting of coherent single-domain rigid sliding that completes within 5 ps, reproducing experimental ferroelectric hysteresis loops.
method: A data-driven framework integrating a MACE machine learning potential with an equivariant graph convolutional neural network (EGCNN) for real-time Born effective charge prediction, combined with real-space path-integral polarization formalism.
model_or_system: AB-stacked bilayer hexagonal boron nitride (h-BN) under applied electric fields.
key_observables: Spontaneous polarization, Born effective charge (BEC), and ferroelectric hysteresis loops.
important_parameters: Switching timescale (5 ps), electric field strength, and AB-stacking configuration.
main_assumptions: The use of a fine-tuned MACE machine learning potential and a specific Gaussian convolution background extraction procedure to isolate intrinsic polarization.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of sliding ferroelectricity, presents a coupled atomistic framework using ML potentials and EGCNN, describes the polarization extraction methodology, and concludes with the simulation of ultrafast switching and hysteresis loops.
